sections.paragraph:
m. (from the root גָּזַז), pl. const. גִּזֵּי.-

(1) [“a shearing, meton.”] shorn wool of sheep, a fleece; Deu 18:4, רֵאשִׁית גֵּז צֹאנְךָ “the firstfruits of the fleece of thy sheep,” Job 31:20 compare גִּזָּה.

(2) [“a mowing, e. g.”] a mown meadow, Psa 72:6 Amo 7:1, גִּזֵּי הַמֶּלֶךְ “the king’s mowings.” This apparently signifies the firstfruits of the hay, which the kings of Israel perhaps required.
